text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Fed's Goolsbee still hawkish on inflation, Williams more dovish — that tug-of-war keeps rate-sensitive tech choppy. Apple's price hike news adds to sticky-inflation fears, which is a headwind for growth/chip names like yours.

Your holdings: PLTR is the loudest problem — down 17.56% and on a SELL signal; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast. AVGO is your largest position, down 13.18% with a TRIM signal — size makes this the biggest dollar risk. SPCX/SPAL are both flashing red today: headline says SpaceX FOMO is officially over and space stocks are getting punished broadly. SPCX is a brand-new IPO with almost no price history — on margin, a volatile new name can gap through your stop before you blink.

Watch: 1) Space sector — any follow-through selling hits SPCX and SPAL hard given SPAL is 2x leveraged. 2) Broader tech tone around inflation prints — sticky inflation = Fed stays tough = more pressure on your chip/growth book.

Bottom line: Your two biggest fires are PLTR and AVGO — size or exit before margin turns a bad week into a margin call.
